title: oxytropis hirta subsp. komarovii
intro: Oxytropis hirta subsp. komarovii, also known as Komarov's oxytropis, is a subspecies of the Oxytropis hirta plant, a perennial flowering plant belonging to the legume family. Traditionally, the roots of Oxytropis hirta subsp. komarovii have been used in folk medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of this plant are believed to include the treatment of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to act as an antifungal agent. Oxytropis hirta subsp. komarovii is typically prepared for medicinal use through the production of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
